metadata.title: HealthProof — Sovereign Medical Verification
metadata.description: Official web client for sovereign medical verification

hero.captionBase: Built for the new era of
hero.captionBaseBold: health data interoperability and ICD-11 digital standards

actors.medicalCenterSummary: Checks permissions and authenticity before attending.
actors.laboratorySummary: Delivers clinical results ready for verification.
actors.patientSummary: You decide who accesses your information and for how long.

solution.title: A verification layer for medical documents
solution.description: Instead of trusting PDFs, emails, or manual validations, every document receives a
solution.descriptionBold: tamper-proof verification record anchored on blockchain.
solution.footer: All without replacing existing hospital systems. HealthProof

regulatory.title: Healthcare is entering the era of verifiable data
regulatory.intro: Healthcare systems are undergoing a structural shift driven by:
regulatory.riskIntro: The risk is no longer losing documents. The risk is
regulatory.riskBold: trusting documents that cannot be verified.
regulatory.alteredPdf: A single altered PDF can change:

useCases.title: Clinical workflows built on verifiable evidence
useCases.splitText: HealthProof makes it possible in three simple steps!

cases.title: Medical Centers
cases.description: Doctors issue medical orders that are cryptographically registered and traceable. When patients move across institutions, the document travels with proof of authenticity.

cases.title: Patients
cases.description: Patients control access to their medical documents. They approve which institution can read or update their exams.

cases.title: Laboratories
cases.description: Laboratories receive verifiable orders and attach test results directly to the original request. Every update is timestamped and auditable.

workflow.title: Verification without exposing medical data

steps.title: Medical order issued
steps.description: A doctor creates a medical test order inside their system. A cryptographic hash of the document is registered on blockchain.

steps.title: Patient authorizes laboratory access
steps.description: The patient grants the laboratory permission to view the order. Authorization can occur via QR code or one-click approval.

steps.title: Laboratory performs the test
steps.description: The laboratory updates the exam status and uploads the results. The new document is linked to the original order.

steps.title: Doctor verifies results
steps.description: The patient returns to the medical center. The doctor verifies the results instantly through HealthProof.

workflow.tagline: The patient does not share data.

technology.title: Proof infrastructure built for healthcare
technology.l1: Each country can operate its own
technology.l1End: to comply with national data sovereignty laws.

icd11.title: Designing the global transition to ICD-11
icd11.introEnd: is the new global standard for digital health data. Adopted by the World Health Organization, ICD-11 enables standardized health information exchange across countries and healthcare systems.
icd11.adapt: Healthcare institutions must adapt their digital infrastructure to support these standards.
icd11.accelerate: HealthProof accelerates that transition by enabling
icd11.accelerateBold: verifiable clinical documentation compatible with interoperable health systems.

finalCta.title: Prepare your institution for verifiable healthcare
finalCta.description: Interoperability is not optional anymore. Healthcare institutions will soon need to prove:
finalCta.footer: HealthProof provides the infrastructure to do that

team.title: HealthProof Co-Founders
team.description: HealthProof is built by a team with experience in
team.descriptionBold: blockchain infrastructure, healthcare innovation, and digital product development.

painBullets: Assume legal responsibility for data leaks or document manipulation

auth.subtitle: Access your medical records securely

roles.patientDesc: Access and share your medical records securely
roles.medicalCenterDesc: Issue orders and verify clinical documents

descriptions.patient: Manage your medical documents, grant or revoke access permissions, and verify the integrity of your clinical records.
descriptions.laboratory: Perform tests linked to medical orders, upload verifiable results, and manage your laboratory workflow.
descriptions.medicalCenter: Issue medical orders, verify laboratory results, and manage your institution's clinical workflows.

actions.title: Actions
actions.shareResultsDesc: Generate a temporary QR code to grant access to a doctor or lab.
actions.myDocumentsDesc: View and manage your encrypted medical documents.
actions.activePermissionsDesc: Review and revoke access permissions you have granted.
actions.auditLogDesc: View the on-chain history of all access events.
actions.connectWalletDesc: Link your wallet to sign permissions cryptographically.
actions.scanQrDescLab: Scan a patient's QR code to receive access to their results.
actions.scanQrDescMc: Verify a patient's identity and access their authorized records.
actions.uploadResultsDesc: Upload encrypted exam results linked to a medical order.
actions.pendingOrdersDesc: View medical orders assigned to your laboratory.
actions.resultsHistoryDesc: Browse all results uploaded by your laboratory.
actions.createOrderDesc: Issue a new medical order for a patient examination.
actions.verifyResultsDesc: Check the integrity and on-chain registration of lab results.
actions.patientRecordsDesc: Access authorized patient records via verified permissions.
actions.manageEpisodesDesc: Open, lookup, and close clinical episodes for patients.
actions.adminPanelDesc: Manage protocol settings, entities, and system administration.
actions.comingSoonDesc: QR scanner will be available when the backend is ready.

profileBanner.title: Complete your profile
profileBanner.description: Add your full name and wallet address to unlock all HealthProof features.

profile.heading: Complete your profile
profile.intro: Fill in your details to unlock the full HealthProof experience. Your embedded wallet was automatically created by Privy.
profile.noWalletHint: A wallet will be created when you need on-chain features.
profile.updatedDesc: Your profile has been saved successfully.

contact.title: Contact — HealthProof
contact.description: Get in touch with the HealthProof team

contact.intro: Have a question about HealthProof, want to explore a partnership, or need technical details? Reach out and our team will respond shortly.
contact.emailRequiredDesc: Please enter a valid email address.

shareModal.title: Share Results
shareModal.description: Generate a temporary QR code to grant access to your medical data.
shareModal.walletSigned: Payload will be signed with your embedded wallet.
shareModal.noWallet: Connect a wallet for signed permissions.
shareModal.qrGeneratedDesc: Signed permission QR for {role} created. Expires in {minutes} minutes.
shareModal.errorDesc: Failed to sign QR payload. Please try again.
shareModal.selectRecipientDesc: Choose who you want to share access with.
shareModal.selectResourceDesc: Choose what type of resource to share.
shareModal.qrShowDescription: Show this QR code to the recipient. It contains a signed permission payload.
shareModal.noResults: No encrypted results found. Results must be uploaded first.
shareModal.selectResultDesc: Choose which exam result you want to share.
shareModal.recipientRequiredDesc: Select a recipient to encrypt the access key for them.
shareModal.noRecipientKey: Recipient has no encryption key. They must log in at least once.
shareModal.noLabKeyFound: Could not determine the original uploader for this result.
shareModal.noLabPublicKey: The lab's encryption key could not be found.
shareModal.noPatientKeys: Your encryption keys were not found. Please log out and log back in.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The route you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved. This may be an invalid URL or a page that hasn't been built yet.
notFound.hint: If you believe this is an error, please contact our team.

serverError.title: Something went wrong
serverError.description: An unexpected error occurred while processing your request. Our infrastructure is designed for resilience — this is likely a temporary issue.
serverError.hint: If the problem persists, please contact our support team.

scanModal.title: Scan QR / Paste Payload
scanModal.description: Paste the QR payload shared by a patient to verify and decrypt their result.
scanModal.pasteRequiredDesc: Paste the QR payload to process it.
scanModal.invalidQrDesc: The payload is not a valid HealthProof permission QR.
scanModal.expiredDesc: This permission QR has expired. Ask the patient for a new one.
scanModal.decryptedDesc: The exam result has been decrypted and is ready to view.
scanModal.processError: Could not decrypt the result. Please try again.

uploadModal.title: Upload Results
uploadModal.description: Select a file to encrypt and upload to IPFS. The file will be encrypted client-side before upload.
uploadModal.dragHint: or drag and drop a file here
uploadModal.clientMode: Client mode: The encrypted file is stored on IPFS via Pinata. Metadata is saved locally. No backend required.
uploadModal.patientRequiredDesc: Select a patient to encrypt the result for them.
uploadModal.noLabKeys: Encryption keys not found. Please log out and log back in to regenerate.
uploadModal.noPatientKey: Patient has no encryption key. The patient must log in at least once to generate their key pair.
